topic: Lemon Grove Incident / Juan De Dios Gonzalez
what: In 1931, Juan De Dios Gonzalez organized a school boycott in Lemon Grove, California, leading to Roberto Alvarez v. Board of Trustees — the first major successful school desegregation ruling in US history. This was 23 years before Brown v. Board of Education.
why_interesting: The commonly told story of school desegregation begins in 1954. But a Mexican-American community won this fight in 1931 and it is barely known. The erasure of precursors from historical memory is a pattern: the 'first' that gets remembered is often the one that fits the dominant narrative, not the actual first.
uncertainty: Why did the Lemon Grove ruling not set a broader legal precedent? Was it a state-level ruling? Did it influence the lawyers who argued Brown v. Board?
